title: PEXPIRE
description: Sets the expiration time of a key in milliseconds.
syntax: PEXPIRE key milliseconds [NX | XX | GT | LT]

redis_py.signature: pexpire(name: KeyT, time: ExpiryT, nx: bool = False, xx: bool = False, gt: bool = False, lt: bool = False)

returns.description: 1 if the timeout was set, 0 otherwise

params.description: can be NX, XX, GT or LT
params.name: expiryOption

returns.description: 1 if the timeout was set, 0 otherwise

returns.description: 1 if the timeout was set, 0 otherwise

params.description: Set expiry only when the key has no expiry.
params.name: nx

params.description: Set expiry only when the key has an existing expiry.
params.name: xx

params.description: Set expiry only when the new expiry is greater than current one.
params.name: gt

params.description: Set expiry only when the new expiry is less than current one.
params.name: lt

returns.description: whether the timeout was set or not

redis_rb.signature: pexpire(key, milliseconds, nx: nil, xx: nil, gt: nil, lt: nil)

content:
This command works exactly like [`EXPIRE`]() but the time to live of the key is specified in milliseconds instead of seconds. ## Options The `PEXPIRE` command supports a set of options since Redis 7.0: * `NX` -- Set expiry only when the key has no expiry * `XX` -- Set expiry only when the key has an existing expiry * `GT` -- Set expiry only when the new expiry is greater than current one * `LT` -- Set expiry only when the new expiry is less than current one A non-volatile key is treated as an infinite TTL for the purpose of `GT` and `LT`. The `GT`, `LT` and `NX` options are mutually exclusive. ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} SET mykey "Hello" PEXPIRE mykey 1500 TTL mykey PTTL mykey PEXPIRE mykey 1000 XX TTL mykey PEXPIRE mykey 1000 NX TTL mykey {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="pexpire-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} One of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if the timeout was not set. For example, if the key doesn't exist, or the operation skipped because of the provided arguments. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the timeout was set. -tab-sep- One of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if the timeout was not set. For example, if the key doesn't exist, or the operation skipped because of the provided arguments. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the timeout was set. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
SET mykey "Hello"
PEXPIRE mykey 1500
TTL mykey
PTTL mykey
PEXPIRE mykey 1000 XX
TTL mykey
PEXPIRE mykey 1000 NX
TTL mykey
intro:
This command works exactly like [`EXPIRE`]() but the time to live of the key is
specified in milliseconds instead of seconds.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
This command works exactly like [`EXPIRE`]() but the time to live of the key is
specified in milliseconds instead of seconds.

sections.title: Options
sections.content:
The `PEXPIRE` command supports a set of options since Redis 7.0:

* `NX` -- Set expiry only when the key has no expiry
* `XX` -- Set expiry only when the key has an existing expiry
* `GT` -- Set expiry only when the new expiry is greater than current one
* `LT` -- Set expiry only when the new expiry is less than current one

A non-volatile key is treated as an infinite TTL for the purpose of `GT` and `LT`.
The `GT`, `LT` and `NX` options are mutually exclusive.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
{{% redis-cli %}}
SET mykey "Hello"
PEXPIRE mykey 1500
TTL mykey
PTTL mykey
PEXPIRE mykey 1000 XX
TTL mykey
PEXPIRE mykey 1000 NX
TTL mykey
{{% /redis-cli %}}

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="pexpire-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

One of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if the timeout was not set. For example, if the key doesn't exist, or the operation skipped because of the provided arguments.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the timeout was set.

-tab-sep-

One of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if the timeout was not set. For example, if the key doesn't exist, or the operation skipped because of the provided arguments.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the timeout was set.

{{< /multitabs >}}
